session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8626 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by calling the erk command to fetch PR feedback and then classify it.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show file restructuring context for PR

**Branch:** `plnd/fix-flaky-timestamp-test-03-02-1831`", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8626#issuecomment-3986145901" }, { "id": 3986220338, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- dignified-code-simplifier-review -->

## ❌ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-02 10:51:04 PT

Found 2 violations across 1 file. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL (Check Before Leap) - No EAFP patterns found
✅ Pathlib usage - All path operations correct
✅ Imports - Absolute imports only
❌ Default parameter values - Found violations in function signatures
✅ Indentation depth - All within limits
✅ Simple ternaries - Properly used
✅ Test determinism - Successfully eliminated flaky datetime.now() calls

### Violations Summary
- `naming.py:722`: Default parameter `now: datetime | None = None` violates api-design rules
- `naming.py:792`: Default parameter `now: datetime | None = None` violates api-design rules

### Files Reviewed
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/naming.py`: 2 violations
- `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/create_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/management/test_impl.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/conftest.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_output_modes.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_plan_file_operations.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/core/utils/test_naming.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-02 10:51:04 PT**: Found 2 violations (default parameter values in naming.py:722, :792) - api-design pattern violation
- **2026-03-02 10:49:42 PT**: All violations resolved - no violations detected in this review
